content:
# Acts Chapter 10 -- The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus, PDF pages 237-238 | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/ACT_010_page_001.jpg, ACT_010_page_002.jpg

---

**1.** And a certain man in Caesarea, by name Cornelius, a centurion of the cohort called the Italian,

**2.** devout and fearing God with all his household, both doing many alms to the people and praying to God continually,

**3.** he saw in a vision plainly, about the ninth hour of the day, an angel of God having come in to him and having said to him, "Cornelius."

**4.** And having gazed at him and having become afraid, he said, "What is [it], Lord?" And he said to him, "Your prayers and your alms have gone up for a memorial before God.

**5.** And now send men to Joppa and send-for a certain Simon who is called Peter.

**6.** This one is lodging with a certain Simon, a tanner, whose house is by [the] sea."

**7.** And when the angel speaking to him had departed, having called two of the household-servants and a devout soldier of those attending him continually,

**8.** and having explained all [things] to them, he sent them to Joppa.

**9.** And on the next day, as those [men] were journeying and drawing near to the city, Peter went up upon the housetop to pray, about [the] sixth hour.

**10.** And he became hungry and was wanting to eat. And while they were preparing, an ecstasy fell upon him,

**11.** and he beholds the heaven having been opened, and a certain vessel descending, as a great linen-sheet, by four corners being let down upon the earth,

**12.** in which were all the four-footed [animals] and creeping-things of the earth and birds of the heaven.

**13.** And a voice came to him, "Having risen, Peter, slaughter and eat."

**14.** But Peter said, "By no means, Lord, because never did I eat anything common and unclean."

**15.** And a voice [came] again a second [time] to him, "What things God has cleansed, you must not make common."

**16.** And this happened upon three [times], and immediately the vessel was taken up into the heaven.

**17.** And as Peter was perplexed in himself [as to] what the vision which he saw might be, behold, the men who had been sent by Cornelius, having inquired-for the house of Simon, stood at the gate,

**18.** and having called out, they were asking whether Simon, the [one] called Peter, is lodging here.

**19.** And while Peter was pondering about the vision, the Spirit said to him, "Behold, men are seeking you.

**20.** But having risen, go down and go with them, doubting nothing, because I have sent them."

**21.** And Peter, having gone down to the men, said, "Behold, I am [the one] whom you are seeking. What [is] the reason for which you are here?"

**22.** And they said, "Cornelius, a centurion, a man righteous and fearing God, and being testified-to by the whole nation of the Jews, was instructed by a holy angel to send-for you to his house and to hear words from you."

**23.** Therefore having called-them-in, he lodged [them]. And on the next day, having risen, he went out with them, and some of the brothers from Joppa went with him.

**24.** And on the next day he entered into Caesarea. And Cornelius was expecting them, having called together his relatives and close friends.

**25.** And as it happened [that] Peter entered, Cornelius, having met him, having fallen at [his] feet, worshiped [him].

**26.** But Peter raised him, saying, "Stand up; I myself also am a man."

**27.** And conversing with him, he entered and finds many having come together.

**28.** And he said to them, "You yourselves know how unlawful it is for a Jewish man to join-himself-to or come-near-to [one] of-another-nation. And to me God showed to call no man common or unclean.

**29.** Therefore also without objection I came, having been sent for. I ask, then, for what reason did you send for me?"

**30.** And Cornelius said, "Four days ago until this hour, I was praying [at] the ninth [hour] in my house, and behold, a man stood before me in bright clothing,

**31.** and he says, 'Cornelius, your prayer has been heard and your alms have been remembered before God.

**32.** Send therefore to Joppa and call-for Simon who is called Peter. This one is lodging in [the] house of Simon, a tanner, by [the] sea.'

**33.** Therefore immediately I sent to you, and you did well having come. Now therefore we all are present before God to hear all the [things] having been commanded to you by the Lord."

**34.** And Peter, having opened [his] mouth, said, "In truth I comprehend that God is not a respecter-of-persons,

**35.** but in every nation the [one] fearing him and working righteousness is acceptable to him.

**36.** The word which he sent to the sons of Israel, proclaiming-good-news [of] peace through Jesus Christ -- this one is Lord of all --

**37.** you yourselves know, the thing having happened throughout all Judea, having begun from Galilee after the baptism which John proclaimed,

**38.** Jesus, the [one] from Nazareth, how God anointed him [with the] Holy Spirit and power, who went about doing good and healing all those being oppressed by the devil, because God was with him.

**39.** And we [are] witnesses of all things which he did both in the land of the Jews and in Jerusalem, whom also they killed, having hanged [him] upon a tree.

**40.** This one God raised on the third day and gave him to become manifest,

**41.** not to all the people, but to witnesses having been chosen-beforehand by God, to us, who ate-with and drank-with him after he rose from [the] dead.

**42.** And he commanded us to proclaim to the people and to testify solemnly that this one is the [one] having been appointed by God [as] judge of living and dead.

**43.** To this one all the prophets bear witness, [that] everyone believing in him receives forgiveness of sins through his name."

**44.** While Peter was still speaking these words, the Holy Spirit fell upon all those hearing the word.

**45.** And the faithful ones of [the] circumcision were astonished, as many as came with Peter, because also upon the Gentiles the gift of the Holy Spirit has been poured out.

**46.** For they were hearing them speaking in tongues and magnifying God. Then Peter answered,

**47.** "Surely no one is able to forbid the water, [so as] for these not to be baptized, who received the Holy Spirit even as also we [did]?"

**48.** And he commanded them to be baptized in the name of Jesus Christ. Then they asked him to remain [for some] days.

---

## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **Verse 3 -- "plainly" (phaneross):** Sinaiticus reads ΦΑΝΕΡΩC, confirming the adverb "plainly/openly" modifying the vision, consistent with the majority reading.

2. **Verse 6 -- Omission of additional clause:** Sinaiticus omits the later Byzantine addition "this one will tell you what you must do" (found in some later manuscripts). The text ends simply with "whose house is by the sea."

3. **Verse 11 -- "linen-sheet" (othonen):** Sinaiticus reads ΟΘΟΝΗΝ, "linen sheet/cloth," which I have rendered as "linen-sheet." Some translations render this simply as "sheet."

4. **Verse 12 -- "four-footed animals and creeping-things of the earth and birds of the heaven":** Sinaiticus omits "and wild beasts" (kai theria), which appears in some later manuscripts. The Sinaiticus reading lists three categories rather than four.

5. **Verse 19 -- Number of men:** Sinaiticus does not specify the number of men in this verse. Some later manuscripts read "three men" (cf. Byzantine text), while Sinaiticus simply reads "men" (ΑΝΔΡΕC).

6. **Verse 20 -- "I have sent them":** Sinaiticus reads EGO APESTALKA AUTOUS, "I have sent them," with the Spirit speaking in the first person as the sender of the men, a theologically significant reading shared with other early witnesses.

7. **Verse 25 -- "as it happened [that] Peter entered":** Sinaiticus reads WC DE EGENETO TOU EICΕΛΘΕΙΝ, using the articular infinitive construction, a characteristically Lukan grammatical form.

8. **Verse 30 -- "Four days ago":** Sinaiticus reads APO TETARTHC HMERAC, literally "from [the] fourth day." The unusual temporal construction is preserved literally.

9. **Verse 33 -- "by the Lord":** Sinaiticus reads ΥΠΟ ΤΟΥ ΚΥΡΙΟΥ ("by the Lord") rather than the variant ΥΠΟ ΤΟΥ ΘΕΟΥ ("by God") found in some manuscripts. This is a significant textual variant.

10. **Verse 36 -- Parenthetical:** The clause "this one is Lord of all" (ΟΥΤΟC ΕCΤΙΝ ΠΑΝΤΩΝ ΚΥΡΙΟC) stands as an appositional or parenthetical statement. The syntax of verses 36-37 is notably rough in the Greek, with an anacoluthon -- the sentence structure shifts midway. I have preserved this awkwardness rather than smoothing it.

11. **Verse 37 -- "the thing having happened" (to genomenon rhema):** Literally "the word/thing having become," with ΡΗΜΑ in the accusative. The construction is unusual and possibly reflects a Semitic idiom.

12. **Verse 44 -- "these words":** Sinaiticus reads ΤΑ ΡΗΜΑΤΑ ΤΑΥΤΑ, "these words/sayings," using the plural of rhema.

13. **Verse 48 -- "in the name of Jesus Christ":** Sinaiticus reads ΕΝ ΤΩ ΟΝΟΜΑΤΙ ΙΗCΟΥ ΧΡΙCΤΟΥ, with the characteristic Sinaiticus nomina sacra abbreviations (IHU XPU). Some manuscripts read "in the name of the Lord" or "in the name of the Lord Jesus Christ."

-- EMET
